channel.title: Management on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Management on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Taking Responsibility As A Lead
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about taking responsibility as a lead.

item.title: Running Meetings
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to talk about running meetings, something I know you all love.

item.title: Institutional Knowledge
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about institutional knowledge, which is sometimes called tribal knowledge, but I don’t like that name.

item.title: Fungible Employees
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to talk about flem.

item.title: Leading For The First Time
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about leading for the first time, In particular, the very first time you’re put in a lead role or even leading an entire project, a game director role.

item.title: Prioritizing Tasks
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about prioritizing tasks.

item.title: Game Development As A Family Car Trip
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game development.

item.title: The Blame Problem
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the blame problem.

item.title: Onboarding New Team Members
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about onboarding new team members.

item.title: Communication Between Roles
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about communication between roles, specifically, how to handle all the different roles of people on your development team and getting them to talk to each other.

item.title: Matching Your Project And Team
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about matching your project and your team.

item.title: Authority Without Responsibility
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Authority without responsibility.

item.title: Morale Improvement
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about morale Improvement.

item.title: The Business Side Of Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the business side of game development.

item.title: How To Remedy Problematic Behavior
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how to remedy problematic behaviors and these are on people on a team you’re on, in fact.

item.title: Working In Teams
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about working in teams — something you will almost inevitably have to do if you go in the game industry — and I want to tell you why I’m talking about this.

item.title: Relinquishing Creative Control
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about relinquishing creative control.

item.title: Delegation
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about delegation, the dreaded delegation.

item.title: Design: Committee vs. Collaboration
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about designed by committee versus design by collaboration.

item.title: Troika's Flat Hierarchy Experiment
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about flat management hierarchies.

item.title: My Biggest Lesson
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer a question about lessons.

item.title: Crunch
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about crunch.

item.title: The Credits Problem (e.g. Fallout Companions)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about credits and how people get credited in games, and I’m going to do that by telling you a story, because I’m frequently asked: hey uh, who did the companions in Fallout?

item.title: How To Build A Motivated Team (1996)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I’m going to read to you a presentation that I made — I think it was sometime in 1996 — at interplay, on how to build a motivated team.
